title: Colysis minor
taxonomy: Colysis minor is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Colysis, which is part of the order Polypodiales. Colysis minor is native to tropical regions and is often found in humid forests. The plant is classified as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows on the ground. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as the presence of pinnate leaves and a rhizome base.

intro: Colysis minor, also known as Minor Colysis, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Colysis minor include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
